questions.question_text: Which among the following is an ambidentate ligand?
questions.question_html: Which among the following is an ambidentate ligand?
questions.explanation: Ambidentate ligands are those ligands that can ligate through two different atoms. Nitrito is an ambidentate ligand as it can coordinate to the central metal atom/ion through O in nitrito-O i.e. ONO - and through N in nitrito-N i.e. NO 2 - .

questions.question_text: Which of the following represents a chelate ligand?
questions.question_html: Which of the following represents a chelate ligand?
questions.explanation: When a di- or polydentate ligand uses its two or more donor atoms to bind to a single metal atom/ion, it is said to be a chelate ligand. Ethane-1,2-diamine (NH 2 CH 2 CH 2 NH 2 ) can act as a chelate ligand as it is a didentate ligand.

questions.question_text: Ligand (en) is an example of
questions.question_html: Ligand (en) is an example of
questions.explanation: A ligand that can bind through two donor atoms is called a didentate ligand like en or ethane-1,2-diamine (NH 2 CH 2 CH 2 NH 2 ) which has two donor N atoms.

questions.question_text: A group of atoms can function as a ligand only when:
questions.question_html: A group of atoms can function as a ligand only when:

options.text: It is a positively charged ion
options.html: It is a positively charged ion

options.text: It is capable of acting as donor of electron pair
options.html: It is capable of acting as donor of electron pair

questions.correct_option_text: It is capable of acting as donor of electron pair
questions.explanation: Ligands are the ions/molecules bound to the central metal atom/ion in a coordination entity. These may be simple ions like Cl - , small molecules like NH 3 , large molecules like ethane-1.2-diamine, or even macromolecules like proteins. A ligand must be able to donate a pair of electrons to the central metal atom/ion and thus they act as Lewis bases.

questions.question_text: The coordination number of Cr in [Cr(NH 3 ) 3 (H 2 O) 3 ]Cl 3 is:
questions.question_html: The coordination number of Cr in [Cr(NH3)3(H2O)3]Cl3 is:
questions.explanation: The coordination number of the central metal atom/ion is equal to the number of neutral molecules or negative ions bound to the central metal atom/ion by non-ionizable secondary linkages. These neutral molecules or negative ions are the ones that are shown inside the square bracket. Here there are 3NH 3 molecules and 3H 2 O molecules associated with Cr inside the bracket. So the coordination number of Cr is 6.

questions.question_text: The oxidation number of Fe in K 4 [Fe(CN) 6 ] is:
questions.question_html: The oxidation number of Fe in K4[Fe(CN)6] is:
questions.explanation: {tex}{/tex} The ligand CN ​​​​​- has charge of –1. So the overall charge carried by 6 CN ​​​​​- ligands is -6. Each potassium ion K + carries a charge of +1. So 4 potassium ions carry an overall charge of +4. This implies that the overall charge on the coordination sphere is -4 to balance the +4 charge of the potassium ions. Let the oxidation number of Fe be x. Then x + (-6) = -4 x = -4 - (-6) x = -4 + 6 x = +2 So, the oxidation number of Fe is +2.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is the most likely structure of CrCl 3 .6H 2 O if 1/3 of total chlorine of the compound is precipitated by adding AgNO 3 to its aqueous solution?
questions.question_html: Which of the following is the most likely structure of CrCl3.6H2O if 1/3 of total chlorine of the compound is precipitated by adding AgNO3 to its aqueous solution?
questions.explanation: In the given molecule, there is 3 Cl present, and ( {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} ) {tex}\times{/tex} 3 = 1 Cl is precipitated per molecule. So, 1 Cl satisfies the primary valence and is ionizable. So 1 Cl will be present outside the coordination sphere. So the correct structure is [Cr(H 2 O) 4 Cl 2 ]Cl.2H 2 O.

questions.question_text: The coordination number of the central ion may be obtained from:
questions.question_html: The coordination number of the central ion may be obtained from:

options.text: The number of ions of opposite charge immediately surrounding the specific ion.
options.html: The number of ions of opposite charge immediately surrounding the specific ion.

options.text: The number of sigma bonds formed by the ligand donor atoms with the central metal atom/ion.
options.html: The number of sigma bonds formed by the ligand donor atoms with the central metal atom/ion.

options.text: The number of pi bonds formed by the ligand donor atoms with the central metal atom/ion.
options.html: The number of pi bonds formed by the ligand donor atoms with the central metal atom/ion.

options.text: The number of ionic bonds formed with the surrounding ions.
options.html: The number of ionic bonds formed with the surrounding ions.

questions.correct_option_text: The number of sigma bonds formed by the ligand donor atoms with the central metal atom/ion.
questions.explanation: Coordination number of the central metal atom/ion is equal to the number of ligand donor atoms directly bonded to the central metal atom/ion. So it can be determined by the number of sigma bonds formed by the ligands with the central metal atom/ion.

questions.question_text: Which of the following complex will give a white precipitate with barium chloride solution?
questions.question_html: Which of the following complex will give a white precipitate with barium chloride solution?
questions.explanation: White coloured BaSO ​​​4 is precipitated on the reaction of the given complex with Barium chloride since SO 4 2- ion is present outside the coordination sphere and is available for reaction with Ba​​​ ​​​​​2+ ion.

questions.question_text: The IUPAC name for [CoCl(NO 2 )(en) 2 ]Cl is:
questions.question_html: The IUPAC name for [CoCl(NO2)(en)2]Cl is:
questions.explanation: In the nomenclature of coordination compounds, the ligands are named in alphabetical order followed by the central metal atom/ion with its oxidation number in roman numeral in parenthesis. The cation is named first and then the anion. Here the complex is a cation. And we can see that the ligands present are Cl - (chlorido), en (ethane-1,2-diamine), and NO 2 - (nitrito-N). The central metal is Cobalt. The anion present is chloride and carries a -1 charge. So the overall charge on the complex cation is +1. Nitrito-N carries -1 charge and chlorido carries -1 charge. en is a neutral ligand. Let's assume the oxidation number for Co to be x. Then x + (-1) +(-1) = +1 x - 2 = +1 x = +1 + 2 = +3 So the oxidation state of Co is +3. Hence the IUPAC name for the given complex is Chloridobis(ethane-1,2-diamine) nitrito-N-cobalt(III) chloride.

questions.question_text: In which of the following compounds the oxidation state of the nickel atom is 0?
questions.question_html: In which of the following compounds the oxidation state of the nickel atom is 0?
questions.explanation: CO is neutral ligand and the overall charge on the complex is 0 so Ni has 0 oxidation state.

questions.question_text: What is the coordination number of chromium in [Cr(NH 3 ) 2 (H 2 O 2 ) 2 ]Cl 3 ?
questions.question_html: What is the coordination number of chromium in [Cr(NH3)2(H2O2)2]Cl3?
questions.explanation: The coordination number of the central metal atom/ion in a complex is the number of ligand donor atoms directly bonded to it. There are 4 ligands attached to Cr so its coordination number is 4.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is non–ionizable?
questions.question_html: Which of the following is non–ionizable?
questions.explanation: In [Co(NH 3 ) 3 Cl 3 ], there is no ion present outside the coordination sphere so it is non-ionizable. All the negative ions/neutral molecules inside the coordination sphere satisfy the secondary valence and are non-ionizable.

questions.question_text: Which of the following pair contains a complex salt and double salt respectively?
questions.question_html: Which of the following pair contains a complex salt and double salt respectively?
questions.explanation: [Cu(NH 3 ) 4 ]SO 4 this is a complex salt because it contains a coordination entity (central metal ion Cu 2+ with 4 ligand molecules of NH 3 in the coordination sphere) while K 2 SO 4 .Al 2 (SO 4 ) 3 .24H 2 O is double salt as it can dissociate completely into simple ions when it is dissolved in water.

questions.question_text: In which of the following compounds, the cemtcen metal atom/ion is in the lowest oxidation state?
questions.question_html: In which of the following compounds, the cemtcen metal atom/ion is in the lowest oxidation state?
questions.explanation: CO is a neutral ligand. The overall charge on the given complex is 0. So, Mn has zero oxidation state.

questions.question_text: The theory which uses the concept of hybridized orbitals to explain the nature of bonding in coordination compounds is:
questions.question_html: The theory which uses the concept of hybridized orbitals to explain the nature of bonding in coordination compounds is:
questions.explanation: According to Valence Bond Theory, the metal atom or ion under the influence of ligands can use its (n-1)d, ns, np or ns, np, nd orbitals for hybridization to yield a set of equivalent orbitals of definite geometry such as octahedral, tetrahedral, square planar and so on. These hybridized orbitals are allowed to overlap with ligand orbitals that can donate electron pairs for bonding. So, the Valence bond theory uses the concept of hybridized orbitals to explain the bonding in complexes.

questions.question_text: The type of hybridization possible with a coordination number of 6 can be:
questions.question_html: The type of hybridization possible with a coordination number of 6 can be:
questions.explanation: sp 3 d 2 hybridization means there are 6 orbitals involved in hybridization (1 ns, 3 np, and 2 nd) and 6 hybrid orbitals are formed. So with coordination number 6, this hybridization is possible.

questions.question_text: The oxidation state of Ag in tollen’s reagent is:
questions.question_html: The oxidation state of Ag in tollen’s reagent is:
questions.explanation: Tollen's reagent contains diamminesilver(I) complex. So oxidation state of Ag in tollen’s reagent is +1.

questions.question_text: Which is not true about a ligand?
questions.question_html: Which is not true about a ligand?

options.text: It can act as a Lewis Base.
options.html: It can act as a Lewis Base.

options.text: A polydentate ligand cannot cause chelation.
options.html: A polydentate ligand cannot cause chelation.

options.text: A monodentate ligand cannot be a chelate ligand.
options.html: A monodentate ligand cannot be a chelate ligand.

options.text: It can be monodentate or polydentate.
options.html: It can be monodentate or polydentate.

questions.correct_option_text: A polydentate ligand cannot cause chelation.
questions.explanation: A polydentate ligand has more than one donor atoms that can bind to the central metal atom/ion. So it can act as a chelate ligand.

questions.question_text: In which of the following complexes, the nickel metal is in the highest oxidation state?
questions.question_html: In which of the following complexes, the nickel metal is in the highest oxidation state?
questions.explanation: The given complex can be written as K 2 [NiF 6 ]. Let the oxidation state of Ni be x. Potassium ion K + carries a +1 charge. So two K + means a total of +2 charge which implies an overall charge of -2 on the complex anion. Each F ​​​​​​- carries -1 charge. So, x + 6(-1) = -2 x - 6 = -2 x = -2 + 6 = +4 So, the oxidation state of Ni is +4 and is the highest among the given complexes.

questions.question_text: KCl.MgCl 2 .6H 2 O is a:
questions.question_html: KCl.MgCl2.6H2O is a:
questions.explanation: A double salt is a salt that crystallizes as a single substance but when it is dissolved it gets ionized as two salts and loses its identity in solution. KCl.MgCl 2 .6H 2 O is a double salt.

questions.question_text: Which of the following can be termed as heteroleptic complex?
questions.question_html: Which of the following can be termed as heteroleptic complex?
questions.explanation: In the given complex, the central metal is bound to more than one donor group. So it is a heteroleptic complex.

questions.question_text: Which of the following ligands gives chelate complexes?
questions.question_html: Which of the following ligands gives chelate complexes?
questions.explanation: Oxalate is a bidentate ligand. So it is a chelate ligand and gives chelate complexes.

questions.question_text: In the formation of complex entity, the central atom/ion acts as:
questions.question_html: In the formation of complex entity, the central atom/ion acts as:
questions.explanation: The central metal atom/ion behaves as a Lewis acid while the ligand acts as a Lewis base. The ligand can be a negatively charged ion or a neutral molecule that donates its electron pair to the central metal atom/ion which acts as an electron pair acceptor (Lewis acid).

questions.question_text: Which of the following is a negatively charged ligand?
questions.question_html: Which of the following is a negatively charged ligand?
questions.explanation: C 5 H 5 - is a cyclopentadienyl anion and it is a negatively charged ligand.

questions.question_text: The correct IUPAC name of Mn 3 (CO) 12 is:
questions.question_html: The correct IUPAC name of Mn3(CO)12 is:
questions.explanation: In the nomenclature of coordination compounds, the ligands are named first followed by the name of central metal atom/ion followed by the oxidation state of the metal in parenthesis. Here CO is the carbonyl ligand, there are 12 CO ligands and they are attached to 3 Mn i.e. Manganese metal atoms. CO is a neutral ligand and carries no charge and hence the oxidation state of Mn is 0. So the IUPAC name is Dodecacarbonyltrimanaganese(0).

questions.question_text: Which is used in cancer therapy?
questions.question_html: Which is used in cancer therapy?
questions.explanation: Cis-platin (cis – [Pt(NH 3 ) 2 (Cl) 2 ]) is a coordination compound used in the treatment of cancer. It inhibits the growth of tumors.

questions.question_text: The correct IUPAC name of [Fe(C 5 H ​​​​5 ) 2 ] is:
questions.question_html: The correct IUPAC name of [Fe(C5H​​​​5)2] is:
questions.explanation: C 5 H 5 - is cyclopentadienyl ligand. The name of the ligand is written first followed by the name of the central metal and its oxidation state in parenthesis in roman numerals. C 5 H 5 - carries -1 charge and there are two such ligands. The overall charge on the complex is 0 so to balance the -2 charge of the ligands iron must be in +2 oxidation state. Hence, the IUPAC name of the complex is Bis(cyclopentadienyl)iron(II).

questions.question_text: Which of the following species is expected to be colourless?
questions.question_html: Which of the following species is expected to be colourless?
questions.explanation: Ti has atomic number 22. And its electronic configuration is 1s 2 2s 2 2p 6 3s 2 3p 6 4s 2 3d 2 . In the given complex, there are four NO 3 ​​​​- groups bonded to Ti. Each NO ​​​​​3 ​​​​- carries -1 charge, hence there is -4 charge on the ligands and overall the complex is neutral which means Ti is in +4 oxidation state. So Ti ​​​​​4+ has an electronic configuration 1s 2 2s 2 2p 6 3s 2 3p 6 means there are no electrons in d orbital and hence d-d transition is not possible. So it is expected to be colourless.

questions.question_text: The correct IUPAC name of K 2 [Zn(OH) 4 ] is:
questions.question_html: The correct IUPAC name of K2[Zn(OH)4] is:
questions.explanation: In an ionic compound, the cation is named first and then the anion. When the complex is anion we add -ate to the central metal. Here the cation is Potassium K + and the anion is the complex. There are 2 K + and hence a total of +2 charge which is balanced by -2 charge on the complex. OH - is hydroxido ligand. Ligand is named first followed by the name of the central metal atom/ion with its oxidation state in parenthesis in roman numerals. Here there are 4 OH - and each carries a -1 charge. Let the oxidation state of Zn be x. Then x + 4(-1) = -2 x - 4 = -2 x = -2 + 4 = +2 So, the oxidation state of Zn=+2 So, the IUPAC name is Potassium tetrahydroxidozincate(II).

questions.question_text: Lithium tetrahydridoaluminate is represented as:
questions.question_html: Lithium tetrahydridoaluminate is represented as:

options.text: Lithium tetrahydridoaluminate is represented as: Li[AlH 4 ]
options.html: Lithium tetrahydridoaluminate is represented as: Li[AlH<sub>4</sub>]

questions.correct_option_text: Lithium tetrahydridoaluminate is represented as: Li[AlH 4 ]
questions.explanation: The cation is named first and then the anion. When the anion is the complex then the -ate is added to the name of the central metal. Here there are 4 hydride ligands represented as H ​​​​​​- each carries a charge of -1 and hence a total of -4 charge on the ligands. Lithium-ion is represented by Li + and carries a +1 charge which means the charge on the complex is -1. In the complex, central metal atom/ion is written followed by the ligands in alphabetical order. Lithium tetrahydridoaluminate(III) is Li[AlH 4 ].

questions.question_text: The correct name of the compound [Cu(NH 3 ) 4 ](NO 3 ) 2 is:
questions.question_html: The correct name of the compound [Cu(NH3)4](NO3)2 is:
questions.explanation: In an ionic compound, the cation is named first and then the anion. If cation is the complex then ligands are named first in alphabetical order and then the central metal atom/ion with its oxidation state in parenthesis in roman numerals. Here, NH 3 (ammine) is the neutral ligand and there are 4 NH 3 bound to Cu (copper). Nitrate NO 3 - is the anion there are two NO 3 - outside the square bracket, each carries -1 charge so there is a total of -2 charge on anions and thus the complex carries a total of +2 charge. Since ammine is a neutral ligand so Cu has +2 oxidation state. So, [Cu(NH 3 ) 4 ](NO 3 ) 2 is tetraamminecopper(II) nitrate.

questions.question_text: Which of the following compound would exhibit coordination isomerism?
questions.question_html: Which of the following compound would exhibit coordination isomerism?
questions.explanation: Coordination isomerism arises from the interchange of ligands between cationic and anionic entities of different metal ions present in a complex. Here interchange of CN - and NH 3 ligands is possible between Cr and Co to give [Co(NH 3 ) 6 ][Cr(CN) 6 ]. So this complex can exhibit coordination isomerism.

questions.question_text: The kind of isomerism exhibited by [Rh(en) 2 Cl 2 ][Ir(en)Cl 4 ] and [Ir(en) 3 ][RhCl 6 ] is:
questions.question_html: The kind of isomerism exhibited by [Rh(en)2Cl2][Ir(en)Cl4] and [Ir(en)3][RhCl6] is:
questions.explanation: When there is the interchange of ligands between the cationic and anion species of different metal ions present in a complex, then coordination isomerism arises. Here we can find the interchange of en and Cl between Ir and Rh in the complexes.

questions.question_text: The metal-carbon bond in metal carbonyls possess:
questions.question_html: The metal-carbon bond in metal carbonyls possess:
questions.explanation: The metal-carbon bond in metal carbonyls possesses both σ and {tex}\pi{/tex} character. The M–C {tex}\sigma{/tex} bond is formed by the donation of lone pair of electrons on the carbonyl carbon into a vacant orbital of the metal. The M–C {tex}\pi{/tex} bond is formed by the donation of a pair of electrons from a filled d orbital of metal into the vacant antibonding {tex}\pi{/tex} * orbital of carbon monoxide. The metal to ligand bonding creates a synergic effect which strengthens the bond between CO and the metal.

questions.question_text: Which of the following complex ions/molecules of nickel is paramagnetic?
questions.question_html: Which of the following complex ions/molecules of nickel is paramagnetic?
questions.explanation: Ni has atomic number 28, so Ni 2+ has electronic configuration 1s 2 2s 2 2p 6 3s 2 3p 6 3d 8 . NH 3 is a weak field ligand and hence two electrons are unpaired and hence this complex is paramagnetic.

questions.question_text: The compounds [Cr(H 2 O) 6 ]Cl 3 and [Cr(H 2 O) 4 Cl 2 ]Cl.2H 2 O exhibits:
questions.question_html: The compounds [Cr(H2O)6]Cl3 and [Cr(H2O)4Cl2]Cl.2H2O exhibits:
questions.explanation: Hydrate isomers differ by whether water molecules are directly bonded to the central metal atom/ion or are merely present as free water molecules in the crystal lattice. In the first complex, the 6 water molecules are directly bonded to the central metal whereas in the second complex 2 water molecules are present as the water of crystallization and 4 water molecules are directly bonded to the central metal. So these exhibit hydrate isomerism.

questions.question_text: Which isomerism is exhibited by the compound [Co(NH 3 ) 3 (H 2 O) 3 ]Cl 3 ?
questions.question_html: Which isomerism is exhibited by the compound [Co(NH3)3(H2O)3]Cl3?
questions.explanation: The given complex is of type [MX 3 Y 3 ] and such complexes exhibit geometrical isomerism. If three donor atoms of the same ligands occupy adjacent positions at the corners of the octahedral face, then we have the facial (fac) isomer. When the positions are around the meridian of the octahedron, we have the meridional (mer) isomer.

questions.question_text: The octahedral complex, trioxalatochromate(III) will show
questions.question_html: The octahedral complex, trioxalatochromate(III) will show
questions.explanation: This complex has non-superimposable mirror images as shown. where M=Cr, n=3 Thus, this complex shows optical isomerism

questions.question_text: Which of the following complexes can form d and l isomers?
questions.question_html: Which of the following complexes can form d and l isomers?
questions.explanation: Cis - [Co(en) 2 Cl 2 ] + has non superimposable mirror images as shown. Hence, it shows optical isomerism and can form d (dextrorotatory) and l (laevorotatory) isomers.

questions.question_text: Which among the following will not show geometrical isomerism?
questions.question_html: Which among the following will not show geometrical isomerism?
questions.explanation: Geometrical isomerism arises in heteroleptic complexes due to different possible geometric arrangements of the ligands. In [Co(en) 3 ] 3+ all ligands are same i.e. ethane-1,2-diamine. It is symmetrical so it does not show geometrical isomerism.

questions.question_text: cis- and trans- isomers are possible in
questions.question_html: cis- and trans- isomers are possible in
questions.explanation: In octahedral complexes of formula [MX 2 L 4 ] in which the two ligands X may be oriented adjacent or opposite to each other, cis and trans isomers are possible. Thus, [Co(NH 3 ) 4 Cl 2 ] + exists as cis and trans isomers.

questions.question_text: What is used as the complexing agent in volumetric analysis of ions like Mg 2+ and Ca 2+ ?
questions.question_html: What is used as the complexing agent in volumetric analysis of ions like Mg2+and Ca2+?
questions.explanation: EDTA (ethylenediaminetetraacetato) is a hexadentate ligand and it forms a stable chelate complex with given metal ions. The selective estimation of these ions can be done due to the difference in stability constants of complexes of these ions with EDTA.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is a homoleptic complex?
questions.question_html: Which of the following is a homoleptic complex?
questions.explanation: Complexes in which the central metal is bound to only one kind of donor groups are called homoleptic complexes. [Cu(NH ​​​​​3 ) 6 ] +2 is a homoleptic complex because in this only ammonia group is the donor group bound to Cu +2 .

questions.question_text: Blue solution of CuSO 4 becomes darker when treated with ammonia because:
questions.question_html: Blue solution of CuSO4 becomes darker when treated with ammonia because:

options.text: Ammonia is stronger ligand than water
options.html: Ammonia is stronger ligand than water

options.text: Ammonia is highly soluble in water
options.html: Ammonia is highly soluble in water

options.text: Ammonia molecule replaces water molecule in solution
options.html: Ammonia molecule replaces water molecule in solution

questions.correct_option_text: Ammonia is stronger ligand than water
questions.explanation: Ammonia forms a stable complex with Cu 2+ which is dark blue in colour. So the blue colour of CuSO 4 solution becomes darker when ammonia is added to it. Cu +2 + 4NH 3 {tex}\to{/tex} [Cu(NH 3 ) 4 ] +2

questions.question_text: According to Werner’s theory, the secondary valences of the central atom correspond to its:
questions.question_html: According to Werner’s theory, the secondary valences of the central atom correspond to its:
questions.explanation: According to Werner’s theory, secondary valences are non-ionizable and are satisfied by neutral molecules or negative ions. Secondary valence is equal to the coordination number and is fixed for a metal.

questions.question_text: K 3 CoF 6 is a high spin complex. What is the hybrid state of Co atom in this complex?
questions.question_html: K3CoF6 is a high spin complex. What is the hybrid state of Co atom in this complex?
questions.explanation: Given complex can be written as K 3 [CoF 6 ]. There are 3 Potassium ions K + means an overall +3 charge on cations and so the charge on the complex anion is -3. Each F - ligand has -1 charge so there is a total of -6 charge on ligands. Let oxidation state of Co (Z=27) be x x + (-6) = -3 x = -3 + 6 = +3 So, the oxidation state of Co=+3 and its electronic configuration is 1s 2 2s 2 2p 6 3s 2 3p 6 3d 6 . Since its, a high spin complex means there is no pairing of electrons in 3d subshell. The coordination number of Co is 6 as there are 6 ligands bound to it, so this octahedral complex has hybridization sp 3 d 2 .

questions.question_text: Tetraaminecopper(II) ion is a square planar complex with one unpaired electron. According to valence bond theory, the hybrid state of copper should be:
questions.question_html: Tetraaminecopper(II) ion is a square planar complex with one unpaired electron. According to valence bond theory, the hybrid state of copper should be:
questions.explanation: Tetraaminecopper(II) ion is square planar. Square planar complexes have dsp ​​​​2 hybridization. So hybridization is dsp 2 .

questions.question_text: The IUPAC name of KAl(SO 4 ) 2 .12H 2 O is:
questions.question_html: The IUPAC name of KAl(SO4)2.12H2O is:
questions.explanation: This is a double salt so its name is written in alphabetical order and the number of molecules of water of crystallisation is mentioned in the end. So the name is aluminium potassium sulphate dodecahydrate.

questions.question_text: The IUPAC name of (NH ​​​​​4 ) 2 Fe(SO ​​​​​4 ) 2 .6H 2 O is:
questions.question_html: The IUPAC name of (NH​​​​​4)2Fe(SO​​​​​4)2.6H2O is:
questions.explanation: This is a double salt so its IUPAC name is written in alphabetical order and the number of the molecules of water of crystallisation is mentioned in the end. So the name is ammonium ferrous sulphate hexahydrate.

questions.question_text: K 3 [Al(C 2 O 4 ) 3 ] is called:
questions.question_html: K3[Al(C2O4)3] is called:
questions.explanation: Cation is named first and then the anion separated by a space. In a coordination complex, the name of the ligand is written first then the central metal atom/ion with its oxidation state in the parenthesis in roman numerals is mentioned. If the complex is an anion then -ate is added to the name of the central metal atom/ion. Here there are 3 K + ions so cations have a charge of +3. So the overall charge on the complex anion is -3. Now each oxalate ion carries -2 charge and there are 3 oxalate ligands attached to aluminium. Let the oxidation state of Al be x. x + 3(-2) = -3 x - 6 = -3 x = -3 + 6 = +3 So, the oxidation state of Al = +3 So, the name of the complex is Potassium trioxalatoaluminate(III).

questions.question_text: Sodium pentacyanonitrosylferrate(II) is also called:
questions.question_html: Sodium pentacyanonitrosylferrate(II) is also called:
questions.explanation: Na 2 [Fe(CN) 5 NO] i.e. Sodium pentacyanonitrosylferrate(II) is also called Sodium nitroprusside.

questions.question_text: The isomers [(C 6 H 5 ) 3 P 2 Pd(SCN) 2 ] and [(C 6 H 5 ) 3 P 2 Pd(NCS) 2 ] show
questions.question_html: The isomers [(C6H5)3P2Pd(SCN)2] and [(C6H5)3P2Pd(NCS)2] show
questions.explanation: SCN ​​​​- is an ambidentate ligand i.e it can bind through two different donor atoms, either through S in SCN ​​​​- or through N in NCS ​​​​- . So it shows linkage isomerism which arises when an ambidentate ligand is present in the complex.

questions.question_text: Ferric chloride combines with potassium ferrocyanide solution to give
questions.question_html: Ferric chloride combines with potassium ferrocyanide solution to give
questions.explanation: This is a confirmatory test to detect Fe +3 ion in unknown sample. Ferric chloride combines with potassium ferrocyanide solution to give blue precipitate of potassium ferri ferrocyanide. FeCl 3 (aq) + K 4 [Fe(CN) 6 ] (aq) {tex}\to{/tex} KFe[Fe(CN) 6 ](s) + 3KCl(aq)

questions.question_text: A complex of platinum, ammonia and chlorine produces four ions per molecule in the solution. The structure consistent with the observation is
questions.question_html: A complex of platinum, ammonia and chlorine produces four ions per molecule in the solution. The structure consistent with the observation is
questions.explanation: On getting ionised this complex gives 3 Cl ​​​​​- (ions outside the square brackets are ionisable) and a [Pt(NH ​​​​​3 ) 3 Cl] + i.e. 4 ions are produced per molecule of the compound.

questions.question_text: Which of the following complex species involves d 2 sp 3 hybridization?
questions.question_html: Which of the following complex species involves d2sp3 hybridization?
questions.explanation: In this complex there are 6 CN - ligands means a total of -6 charge on ligands. There is a charge of -3 on the complex so oxidation state of Fe is +3. Atomic number of Fe is 26. So the electronic configuration of Fe +3 is 1s 2 2s 2 2p 6 3s 2 3p 6 3d 3 .Coordination number of the metal is 6 so the complex has octahedral geometry and since CN - is a strong field ligand so it causes pairing and hence inner orbital complex is formed. So the hybridization is d 2 sp 3 .

questions.question_text: A complex involving dsp 2 hybridisation has
questions.question_html: A complex involving dsp2 hybridisation has
questions.explanation: dsp 2 hybridisation results in a square planar geometry.

questions.question_text: In the complex Fe(CO) x , the value of x is
questions.question_html: In the complex Fe(CO)x , the value of x is
questions.explanation: The complex of Fe with CO ligand is [Fe(CO) 5 ] i.e. pentacarbonyliron(0). So the value of x =5.

questions.question_text: Which among the following has trigonal bipyramidal geometry?
questions.question_html: Which among the following has trigonal bipyramidal geometry?
questions.explanation: Iron (Z=26) has an electronic configuration 1s 2 2s 2 2p 6 3s 2 3p 6 3d 6 4s 2 . CO being a strong field ligand, causes pairing of electrons in the d orbital and shifting of 4s electrons to 3d orbital. With a coordination number 5 it results in sp 3 d hybridisation and hence a trigonal bipyramidal geometry.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is paramagnetic?
questions.question_html: Which of the following is paramagnetic?
questions.explanation: In given complex, there are 3 K + ions and so the anion has overall -3 charge. In [Fe(CN) 6 ] 3 - , there are 6CN - ligands and an overall charge of -3 on the complex and hence Fe is in +3 oxidation state so its electronic configuration is {tex}1s^2 2s^2 2p^6 3s^2 3 p^6 3d^5{/tex} . Since CN - is a strong field ligand and it causes pairing of electrons. But there is one unpaired electron which makes this complex paramagnetic.

questions.question_text: The hybridization of nickel in tetracarbonyl nickel is
questions.question_html: The hybridization of nickel in tetracarbonyl nickel is
questions.explanation: In Ni(CO) 4 , Ni is in 0 oxidation state and its electronic configuration is 1s 2 2s 2 2p 6 3s 2 3d 8 3d 8 4s 2 . CO is a strong field ligand and causes pairing of electrons and shifting of 4s electrons to 3d orbital. So hybridisation of Ni is sp 3 .

questions.question_text: In the complex [Ni(H 2 O) 2 (NH 3 ) 4 ] 2+ ,the number of unpaired electron is
questions.question_html: In the complex [Ni(H2O)2(NH3)4]2+,the number of unpaired electron is
questions.explanation: In given complex both H 2 O and NH​​​​​ ​​​​​3 are weak field ligands and are neutral. So Ni has oxidation state +2. Its electronic configuration is 1s 2 2s 2 2p 6 3s 2 3p 6 3d 8 . So the number of unpaired electrons are 2.

questions.question_text: According to Werner’s theory , the primary valences of the central atom
questions.question_html: According to Werner’s theory , the primary valences of the central atom

options.text: Decide the geometry of the complex
options.html: Decide the geometry of the complex

options.text: Are equal to its coordination number
options.html: Are equal to its coordination number

options.text: Are satisfied by negative ions or neutral molecules
options.html: Are satisfied by negative ions or neutral molecules

questions.explanation: Primary valence of the central metal atom/ion is ionisable and is satisfied by negative ions.

questions.question_text: The crystal field splitting depends upon
questions.question_html: The crystal field splitting depends upon

options.text: Both the field produced by the ligand and the charge on the metal ion.
options.html: Both the field produced by the ligand and the charge on the metal ion.

options.text: Distance of ligand from metal ion
options.html: Distance of ligand from metal ion

questions.correct_option_text: Both the field produced by the ligand and the charge on the metal ion.
questions.explanation: The splitting of degenerate d orbitals in presence of ligands in a definite geometry is called crystal field splitting. The extent of the splitting depends upon the charge on the metal ion and field of the ligand. Some ligands produce strong field and cause a large splitting whereas some ligands produce weak field and cause a small splitting.

questions.question_text: In the complex PtCl 4 .3NH 3 , the number of ionisable chlorines is
questions.question_html: In the complex PtCl4.3NH3, the number of ionisable chlorines is
questions.explanation: The complex [PtCl 3 (NH 3 ) 3 ]Cl has only one ionizable chlorine

questions.question_text: The formula of Zeise’s salt is
questions.question_html: The formula of Zeise’s salt is
questions.explanation: Potassium trichloro(ethylene)platinate(II) i.e. K[PtCl 3 (C 2 H 4 )] i s zeise's salt.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is true about crystal field theory?
questions.question_html: Which of the following is true about crystal field theory?

options.text: It is based on the concept of hybridisation of orbitals.
options.html: It is based on the concept of hybridisation of orbitals.

options.text: It considers the covalent character of bonding between the ligand and the central metal.
options.html: It considers the covalent character of bonding between the ligand and the central metal.

options.text: It explains why anionic ligands are at the low end of the electrochemical series.
options.html: It explains why anionic ligands are at the low end of the electrochemical series.

questions.explanation: The crystal field theory is an electrostatic model which considers the metal-ligand bond to be ionic arising purely from electrostatic interactions between the metal ion and the ligand.

questions.question_text: Wilkinson catalyst is
questions.question_html: Wilkinson catalyst is

options.text: Al(C 2 H 5 ) 3 , TiCl 4 and (C 6 H 5 ) 3 P

questions.explanation: [((C 6 H 5 ) 3 P) 3 RhCl] is Wilkinson catalyst, it is used in hydrogenation of alkenes.

questions.question_text: The hardness of water is estimated by
questions.question_html: The hardness of water is estimated by
questions.explanation: Hardness of water is because of presence of Ca 2+ and Mg 2+ ions which can form stable complexes with EDTA. So by simple titration with EDTA, hardness of water can be estimated. The selective estimation of these ions can be done due to difference in the stability constants of their complexes with EDTA.

questions.question_text: A reagent used for identifying nickel ion is
questions.question_html: A reagent used for identifying nickel ion is
questions.explanation: Ni forms a bright red complex with dimethylglyoxime. This reagent is used in qualitative analysis to identify Ni ​​​​​2+ ion in a given unknown sample.

questions.question_text: In the complex ion, [Fe(C 2 ​​​​​O ​​​​4 ) 3- ], the coordination number of Fe is
questions.question_html: In the complex ion, [Fe(C2​​​​​O​​​​4)3-], the coordination number of Fe is
questions.explanation: Oxalate i.e. C 2 O 4 2- is a didentate ligand as it can bind through 2 O atoms. In given complex there are 3 oxalate ligands bound to Fe. So that means Fe has a coordination number 6.

questions.question_text: In a square planar complex of formula [MX 2 L 2 ] (X and L are unidentate), the two ligands X may be arranged adjacent to each other in a:
questions.question_html: In a square planar complex of formula [MX2L2] (X and L are unidentate), the two ligands X may be arranged adjacent to each other in a:
questions.explanation: In a square planar complex of formula [MX 2 L 2 ] (X and L are unidentate), the two ligands X may be arranged adjacent to each other in a cis-isomer. If they are arranged opposite to each other it becomes a trans isomer.

questions.question_text: The theory which uses the concept of hybridized orbitals to explain the nature of bonding in coordination compounds is:
questions.question_html: The theory which uses the concept of hybridized orbitals to explain the nature of bonding in coordination compounds is:
questions.explanation: According to Valence Bond Theory, the metal atom or ion under the influence of ligands can use its (n-1)d, ns, np or ns, np, nd orbitals for hybridization to yield a set of equivalent orbitals of definite geometry such as octahedral, tetrahedral, square planar and so on. These hybridized orbitals are allowed to overlap with ligand orbitals that can donate electron pairs for bonding. So, the Valence bond theory uses the concept of hybridized orbitals to explain the bonding in complexes.

questions.question_text: Which of the following represents a chelate ligand?
questions.question_html: Which of the following represents a chelate ligand?
questions.explanation: When a di- or polydentate ligand uses its two or more donor atoms to bind to a single metal atom/ion, it is said to be a chelate ligand. Ethane-1,2-diamine (NH 2 CH 2 CH 2 NH 2 ) can act as a chelate ligand as it is a didentate ligand.

questions.question_text: Blue solution of CuSO 4 becomes darker when treated with ammonia because:
questions.question_html: Blue solution of CuSO4 becomes darker when treated with ammonia because:

options.text: Ammonia is stronger ligand than water
options.html: Ammonia is stronger ligand than water

options.text: Ammonia is highly soluble in water
options.html: Ammonia is highly soluble in water

options.text: Ammonia molecule replaces water molecule in solution
options.html: Ammonia molecule replaces water molecule in solution

questions.correct_option_text: Ammonia is stronger ligand than water
questions.explanation: Ammonia forms a stable complex with Cu 2+ which is dark blue in colour. So the blue colour of CuSO 4 solution becomes darker when ammonia is added to it. Cu +2 + 4NH 3 {tex}\to{/tex} [Cu(NH 3 ) 4 ] +2
